free_text: Fragment angular distributions in the 14 MeV neutron
Electroplated onto gold plated Al discs 3mm in diameter
weight=10-50 microg (120 microg in the case of U-235)
Direct counting of fission fragment tracks
Lexan solid-state track detector held in an
Fragment angular distributions were calculated from the
measured track densities at various laboratory angles.
Anisotropies W(0 deg)/W(90 deg) were calculated from a
least squares fit of the experimental points.
Effect of target thickness on the shape of the angular
distribution and on the anisotropy was looked into by
measuring the angular distribution of fragments from a
thin U-235 source (about 100 microg/cm2) and comparing
that with the 1.7 mg/cm2 source (120 microg). It was
found that shape and anisotropy depend on target
thickness,with an anisotropy increase with thickness.
Measured angular distributions were normalized to the
total fission cross section taken from a previous work
